headlineSummary: Anthropic drew the more negative discussion, with some praise but more complaints about excessive code and failed edits, while OpenAI had limited evidence and a mixed-neutral read from a single bad-result comment [E1][E2][E3][E4][E5].

ranking.summary: Anthropic’s discussion skewed negative: one comment praised Claude as a strong orchestrator, but several others criticized it for generating too much code and for failing to implement edits cleanly [E1][E2][E3][E4].
OpenAI’s signal was based on limited evidence and stayed mixed-neutral, with one comment reporting pretty bad results from gpt-realtime [E5].

evidence.excerpt: while nearly everyone is building orchestrators i also noticed claude is already the best orchestrator for claude.

annotations.rationale: The commenter explicitly praises Claude as the best orchestrator for this use case.

evidence.excerpt: when Claude generates copious amounts of code, it makes it way harder to review than small snippets one at a time.

annotations.rationale: The commenter explicitly criticizes Claude for producing too much code, making review harder.

evidence.excerpt: But with current models I can only see this approach as exacerbating the problem; coding agents solution is almost always "more code", not less. Makes for a nice demo, but I can't imagine this would build anything that wouldn't have huge operational problems and 10x-100x more code than necessary.

annotations.rationale: In the Claude Code Swarms story context, the commenter strongly criticizes the approach as producing excessive code and operational problems.

evidence.excerpt: EDIT 2: the garrytan con helped me got past the model refusing to make edits, but it's failing when it attempts to implement them.

annotations.rationale: Within the Shared Claude story context, the commenter reports the model refusing edits and failing implementation attempts.

evidence.excerpt: I've had pretty bad results from gpt-realtime and that's a proprietary model

annotations.rationale: The commenter directly says they had bad results from gpt-realtime, a GPT-branded OpenAI model.
